attractions.name: Lake Norman
attractions.description: Lake Norman is the largest man-made body of fresh water in North Carolina, created between 1959 and 1964 as part of the construction of the Cowans Ford Dam. Often referred to as the "Inland Sea," it features over 520 miles of shoreline and provides a vast playground for boating, fishing, and outdoor recreation. The surrounding area includes charming towns like Cornelius, Davidson, and Huntersville, offering a blend of natural beauty and suburban amenities.

attractions.tips: The Visitor Center is currently closed every Wednesday and Thursday, so plan your trip accordingly if you need staff assistance. Arrive early on summer weekends as the swimming beach and parking lots often reach capacity. Pets are allowed in the park on a leash but are prohibited from the swimming beach and park buildings.

attractions.name: U.S. National Whitewater Center (Whitewater.org)
attractions.description: The U.S. National Whitewater Center is a world-class outdoor recreation facility and non-profit organization located on 1,300 acres along the Catawba River in Charlotte, North Carolina (Whitewater.org). It features the world's largest manmade recirculating whitewater river, offering Class II-IV rapids for rafting and kayaking alongside over 50 miles of trails (Wikipedia). Visitors can also experience rock climbing, zip lines, ropes courses, and seasonal ice skating, as well as live music festivals throughout the year (Axios).

attractions.description: Uwharrie National Forest is a diverse outdoor destination in central North Carolina, featuring rolling hills, ancient mountains, and the expansive Badin Lake. It offers a wide range of activities including hiking, off-roading, and camping within its 50,000+ acres of mixed pine and hardwood forests (U.S. Forest Service). As one of the state's four national forests, it provides a rugged escape just about an hour's drive from the Charlotte area (Wikipedia).

attractions.tips: Purchase OHV trail passes in advance at local vendors like Eldorado Outpost or the District Office as there are no on-site pay stations (RiderPlanet USA). Always check the official website for seasonal closures, particularly for the OHV trails which typically close from mid-December to the end of March (U.S. Forest Service).

The Frog Bog outdoor area is expected to be free to the public.

The neighborhood is renowned for its historic Craftsman-style bungalows, stately oak-lined streets, and vibrant commercial corridors where local businesses operate out of renovated historic homes (Wikipedia), Dilworth Community Association).
attractions.hours: Neighborhood: 24/7; Latta Park: 8:00 AM - 8:00 PM Daily (Charlottes Got A Lot)
attractions.admission: Free (Charlottes Got A Lot)
highlights: ['Latta Park: A historic 20-acre urban oasis featuring walking trails, playgrounds, and sports courts.', 'Historic Architecture: A significant collection of well-preserved Craftsman bungalows and Queen Anne Victorian homes.', "East Boulevard: The neighborhood's primary thoroughfare, known for its unique shops and local dining in converted residences.", 'Cathedral of Saint Patrick: A stunning Neo-Gothic landmark and the mother church of the Roman Catholic Diocese of Charlotte.', 'Mature Tree Canopy: Towering century-old oak trees that create a picturesque green tunnel throughout the streets.', 'Annual Community Events: Popular traditions like the Dilworth Home Tour and the Jubilee festival.']
attractions.tips: The neighborhood is best explored on foot or by bicycle to truly appreciate the front-porch architecture and garden landscapes. Many of the most popular restaurants are located on East Boulevard; try visiting on a weekday to avoid the busiest weekend crowds. (Dilworth Center)
attractions.nearby: South End, Uptown Charlotte, Freedom Park, Myers Park (Wikipedia))

attractions.name: Freedom Park
attractions.description: Freedom Park is a 98-acre urban park located between Charlotte's historic Dilworth and Myers Park neighborhoods. It is centered on a 7-acre lake and offers a variety of recreational activities, including sports fields, paved trails, and a large pavilion for performances (Wikipedia)).
attractions.hours: 7:00 AM - 7:00 PM Daily (Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Free (Expedia)
highlights: ['7-acre lake with a paved trail for walking and running', '12 tennis courts, basketball courts, and soccer fields', 'Antique 2-8-0 steam engine available for children to explore', 'Mahlon Adams Indoor Pavilion for events and gatherings', 'Site of the annual five-day Festival in the Park each September', 'Connection to the Little Sugar Creek Greenway']
attractions.tips: The park is a popular spot for jogging and dog walking. Visitors can also enjoy free films and musical performances at the pavilion during the summer months (Wikipedia)).
attractions.nearby: Charlotte Nature Museum, Discovery Place Nature, Dilworth neighborhood, Myers Park neighborhood, Little Sugar Creek Greenway (Wikipedia))

attractions.name: Myers Park Neighborhood & Historic District
attractions.description: Myers Park is a prestigious historic neighborhood and district in Charlotte, North Carolina, renowned for its masterful landscape design by John Nolen. Established in 1911 as one of the city's first streetcar suburbs, it is famous for its towering 100-year-old willow oak canopy, winding drives, and grand Colonial Revival and Tudor-style homes. The neighborhood remains one of Charlotte's most sought-after residential areas, offering a blend of historic charm and modern luxury. Wikipedia), Charlottes Got A Lot
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (Residential Neighborhood)
attractions.admission: Free (Public Neighborhood)
highlights: ['Stunning canopy of century-old willow oak trees lining major boulevards like Queens Road.', 'Historic architecture featuring masterfully preserved 1920s Colonial Revival and Tudor homes.', "The 'Booty Loop,' a popular 2.85-mile route for walking, running, and cycling.", 'Home to Queens University of Charlotte and the historic Duke Mansion.', 'Proximity to the Little Sugar Creek Greenway and the beautiful Freedom Park.', 'Wing Haven Gardens and Bird Sanctuary, a peaceful public garden within the neighborhood.']
attractions.tips: Take a drive or walk down Queens Road West to experience the full beauty of the oak canopy and grand estates. Visit during the spring for blooming gardens or fall for vibrant foliage. The neighborhood is very pedestrian-friendly, but parking can be limited in residential spots, so look for public lots near retail areas like the Myers Park Center. Charlottes Got A Lot
attractions.nearby: Freedom Park, Little Sugar Creek Greenway, Mint Museum Randolph, SouthPark Mall, Dilworth Neighborhood.

attractions.name: Queens University of Charlotte
attractions.description: Queens University of Charlotte is a private university located in the heart of the historic Myers Park neighborhood. Known for its picturesque campus, it features century-old trees, winding walkways, and a commitment to a student-centered learning experience (Queens University of Charlotte). The university provides a serene environment with close proximity to the vibrant cultural and financial hubs of Uptown Charlotte (Yelp).
attractions.hours: Monday: 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM; Tuesday: 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM; Wednesday: 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM; Thursday: 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM; Friday: 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM; Saturday: Closed (Business Offices); Sunday: Closed (Business Offices) (Note: Facility hours such as the Levine Center and Campus Store may differ) (Queens University of Charlotte, Queens University of Charlotte)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Sunday (Campus grounds generally accessible; business offices vary) (Queens University of Charlotte)
attractions.admission: Free for visitors and campus tours (Queens University of Charlotte)
highlights: 'Nestled in the historic and scenic Myers Park neighborhood ([Queens University of Charlotte)', 'Home to the state-of-the-art Levine Center for Wellness and Recreation (Queens University of Charlotte)', 'Offers daily guided campus tours led by student ambassadors (Queens University of Charlotte)', 'Beautiful grounds featuring lush greenways and winding pedestrian paths (Queens University of Charlotte)', 'Prime location just 10 minutes from both Uptown Charlotte and SouthPark (Yelp)']
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to schedule campus tours in advance through the admissions portal. For parking, guests should check for designated visitor spots or coordinated event parking, as on-street parking on Selwyn Avenue is subject to specific timing restrictions (Queens University of Charlotte, Queens University of Charlotte).
attractions.nearby: Freedom Park, Mint Museum Randolph, Discovery Place Nature, SouthPark Mall, Little Sugar Creek Greenway (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Selwyn Avenue Pub (Official Website)
attractions.description: Selwyn Avenue Pub is a neighborhood staple in Charlotte's Myers Park area, operating out of a charming converted old house since 1990. The venue is renowned for its large outdoor patio, signature 'Transfusion' cocktails, and its status as a premier destination for sports fans to gather and watch games in a casual, high-energy environment (Official Website, Axios Charlotte).
attractions.hours: Mon: Closed; Tue-Thu: 4:00 PM - 11:00 PM; Fri: 3:30 PM - 1:00 AM; Sat: 11:30 AM - 1:00 AM; Sun: 11:30 AM - 11:00 PM (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (No cover charge typically required for entry) (Yelp)
highlights: "Signature 'Transfusion' cocktails (vodka, ginger ale, and grape juice) ([Official Website)", 'Large outdoor patio with an ancient oak tree, fire pits, and big-screen TVs (Charlotte Real Estate Voice)', 'Extensive pub menu featuring Nashville Hot Fries, wings, and acclaimed thin-crust pizzas (Official Website)', 'Popular local hangout for viewing major sporting events, especially Carolina Panthers and college basketball (Official Website)', 'Host for private events, including corporate functions and wedding festivities (Official Website)']
attractions.tips: The patio is the place to be; it features heaters and a retractable roof for inclement weather. Be prepared for a lively, often crowded atmosphere during game days or on weekend nights (Tripadvisor, Yelp).
attractions.nearby: Queens University of Charlotte, Freedom Park, Shain Art Gallery, Park Road Shopping Center (Charlotte Communities Online)

attractions.name: The Penguin Drive-In
attractions.description: The Penguin Drive-In is a historic Charlotte icon that originally opened in 1954 and was reborn in the Dilworth neighborhood in 2022. Known for its "Everybody's A Winner" vibe, the restaurant serves classic American comfort food, including famous fried pickles, stacked burgers, and hearty subs, complemented by a full bar and a 100% free arcade.
attractions.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM - 8:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free (Restaurant)
highlights: ['Famous Fried Pickles and Stacked Burgers', '100% Free On-Site Arcade for all ages', 'Spacious, Dog-Friendly Covered Patio', 'Iceberg Lounge for game-day viewing with big-screen TVs', 'Full-service Elephant Bar with daily drink specials', 'Trivia Wednesdays at 7:00 PM']
attractions.tips: The restaurant is very family and pet-friendly, especially on the covered patio. Be sure to try the $14.99 Lunch Combo and don't miss out on the free arcade games while you wait for your food. Wednesdays are popular for Trivia Night starting at 7:00 PM.
attractions.nearby: Freedom Park, Latta Park, Discovery Place Nature, Dilworth Shopping District

attractions.name: Truliant Amphitheater (formerly PNC Music Pavilion)
attractions.description: Truliant Amphitheater (formerly PNC Music Pavilion) is a premier outdoor concert venue in Charlotte, North Carolina, known for hosting large-scale musical acts and summer tours since 1991 (Wikipedia). With a capacity of approximately 18,000, it features a mix of covered pavilion seating and an expansive general admission lawn (Charlotte Meetings). The venue is a staple of the region's live music scene, attracting top artists across genres like rock, country, and pop (Official Website).
attractions.hours: Box Office: Open on event days starting at 12:00 PM (Official Website). Gates: Typically open 60–90 minutes prior to showtime (Official Website). Parking Lots: Early parking often opens at 2:00 PM; general parking opens 2–3 hours before showtime (Official Website).
attractions.days_open: Open on event days during the concert season (typically spring through fall) (Official Website).
attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by event, typically starting around $10–$40 for lawn seating and ranging up to several hundred dollars or more for premium reserved seats and VIP packages (SeatGeek, Vivid Seats). Standard parking is often included with the ticket price (Official Website).
highlights: 'Expansive 18,000-person capacity venue with both pavilion and lawn seating ([Wikipedia).', 'Exclusive Truliant VIP Club offering table service, private bar, and premium restrooms (Official Website).', 'State-of-the-art sound and lighting systems for high-quality live performances (Live Nation).', 'Designated driver program providing free fountain drinks to responsible fans (Official Website).', 'Mobile-only entry and a fully cash-free venue for streamlined guest services (Official Website).', 'On-site sustainability initiatives, including extensive recycling programs throughout the venue (Official Website).']
attractions.tips: Arrive early to navigate heavy traffic and ensure entry before showtime (Official Website). Note that outside lawn chairs are not permitted, but rentals are available inside (Official Website). Download tickets to your phone in advance as the venue uses mobile-only entry (Official Website). Bringing a small, clear bag is recommended to speed up security checks (Official Website).
attractions.nearby: UNC Charlotte Botanical Gardens, Reedy Creek Nature Center and Preserve, Charlotte Flying Saucer, and the University City entertainment district (Tripadvisor).

attractions.name: University of North Carolina at Charlotte (UNC Charlotte)
attractions.description: The University of North Carolina at Charlotte is a public research university that offers a wide range of doctoral, master's, and bachelor's degree programs through nine colleges. It is recognized as an R1 research institution and features a 1,000-acre wooded main campus along with a Center City location in Uptown Charlotte. Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Botanical Gardens: Daily, daylight hours; McMillan Greenhouse: Monday-Friday, 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Main Campus Buildings: Generally Monday-Friday, 7:00 AM - 7:30 PM (restricted access on weekends) Botanical Gardens, Safety & Security
attractions.admission: Free (General admission to Botanical Gardens and Greenhouse is free; event registration may require a fee) Botanical Gardens
highlights: ['UNC Charlotte Botanical Gardens (Susie Harwood Garden and Van Landingham Glen)', 'McMillan Greenhouse with exotic plant collections', 'J. Murrey Atkins Library featuring over 3.3 million volumes', 'The Dubois Center at UNC Charlotte Center City in Uptown Charlotte', 'R1 research status with high research productivity', 'Niner Nation spirit and active student life at Popp Martin Student Union']
attractions.tips: Visitors can use the Lynx Blue Line light rail for easy access to the Center City campus; parking is available in visitor decks and ParkMobile lots across campus. Visitor Parking, Wikipedia
attractions.nearby: Shoppes at University Place, Reedy Creek Nature Center and Preserve, PNC Music Pavilion, Concord Mills Mall Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Davidson College
attractions.description: Davidson College is a highly selective private liberal arts college located north of Charlotte, North Carolina. Known for its strong academic community and unique transdisciplinary approach, the campus offers a beautiful setting for visitors to explore academic opportunities and student life.
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM & 2:00 PM; Tuesday: 9:15 AM; Wednesday: 2:00 PM; Thursday: 9:15 AM; Friday: 9:00 AM & 2:00 PM; Saturday: 10:00 AM (Select dates only) Davidson College Admission
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Saturdays available on select dates) Davidson College Visit
attractions.admission: Free (Campus visits and information sessions are free of charge) Davidson College Admission
highlights: ['Guided 75-minute campus tours led by current students', 'Information sessions (45 minutes) covering academics and financial aid', 'The Jay Hurt Hub for Innovation & Entrepreneurship', 'Over 100 annual performances and exhibitions through Arts at Davidson', 'NCAA Division I athletics featuring the Davidson Wildcats', 'A scenic campus Arboretum and self-guided walking tours']
attractions.tips: Register for a campus visit at least three weeks in advance, especially for groups. While visiting, stroll down Main Street to explore local businesses and grab 'Davidson swag' at the college bookstore. Use the interactive map for a self-guided experience if you miss a guided tour.
attractions.nearby: Lake Norman, Downtown Davidson Shops, Birkdale Village, Roosevelt Wilson Park, Fisher Farm

attractions.name: Latta Nature Preserve
attractions.description: Latta Nature Preserve is Mecklenburg County's largest nature preserve, encompassing 1,480 acres of green space on a peninsula extending into Mountain Island Lake (Visit Lake Norman). It serves as a vital environmental education hub featuring the Quest Nature Center and the Carolina Raptor Center, protecting diverse habitats and endangered plant species (Carolina Raptor Center).
attractions.hours: Nature Preserve: Daily, sunup to sundown. Quest Nature Center: Mon-Sat 9 a.m. - 5 p.m., Sun 1 p.m. - 5 p.m. Raptor Trail: Daily 10 a.m. - 4 p.m. (Mecklenburg County, Carolina Raptor Center).
attractions.days_open: Daily (Nature Preserve); Monday - Sunday (Quest Nature Center and Raptor Trail) (Mecklenburg County).
attractions.admission: Free admission to Latta Nature Preserve and Quest Nature Center (Quest). Carolina Raptor Center (within the preserve) admission: Adults (23-64) $16, Students (13-22) $14, Seniors/Military/Teachers $14, Children (3-12) $12, Under 3 Free (Carolina Raptor Center).
attractions.phone: 980-314-7878 (Quest Nature Center), 704-875-6521 (Carolina Raptor Center) (Mecklenburg County, Carolina Raptor Center).
highlights: '16 miles of trails for hiking and horseback riding ([Visit Lake Norman)', 'Quest Nature Center featuring a 6,500-gallon native fish aquarium (Mecklenburg County Blog)', 'Carolina Raptor Center, a world-renowned bird of prey rehabilitation and education facility (Carolina Raptor Center)', 'Buzzard Rock Overlook providing panoramic views of Mountain Island Lake (Charlotte On The Cheap)', 'Two kayak and canoe launch areas for access to Mountain Island Lake (Mecklenburg County)', 'Extensive birdwatching and wildlife observation opportunities (Visit Lake Norman)']
attractions.tips: Parking and admission to the preserve and Quest center are free, but the Carolina Raptor Center requires a separate paid ticket (Mecklenburg County Blog). Bring your own kayak or paddleboard to use the public launches, and ensure dogs remain on a leash at all times on the trails (Visit Lake Norman).
attractions.nearby: Historic Latta Place (on-site, reopening 2026), Mountain Island Lake, David B. Waymer Recreation and Senior Center (Cornelius Today).
